animation: veo3_1_lite via HF hybrid (16:9); cross falls back to direct-Kling (NSFW)
style_base: Baroque oil painting, dramatic chiaroscuro, Caravaggio/Rembrandt lighting, deep shadow and warm golden light, reverent sacred art, muted earth tones, fine brushwork, no text, no modern elements, 16:9 cinematic wide
rule: every quote's visual matches its soundstage cue; the cut CLOSES on Christ (S22 hero). Cross/crucifixion scenes are ROBED with pierced hands shown (veo NSFW workaround).

scenes.title: The wilderness, the prophet
scenes.subject_block: a monumental lone Hebrew prophet standing on a windswept rocky clifftop ridge at storm-dawn, viewed from slightly below for grandeur and scale, his long robe and mantle whipping hard in the wind, both hands gripping a great unfurled ancient scroll that snaps and billows in the gale, an immense churning storm sky split by one dramatic shaft of golden light breaking through black clouds onto him, a vast deep valley falling away far below, awe and foreboding, towering cinematic scale, NO cross, NO modern elements

scenes.title: The scroll, the question
scenes.subject_block: a single seamless full-bleed Baroque oil painting, hard chiaroscuro: close on a weathered ancient hand resting on an unrolled parchment scroll by warm candlelight, the scroll covered in faint indistinct ancient script with no readable or legible letters, a reed pen and an ink pot, deep shadow, a question hanging in the dark; one continuous image, no frame, no panels, no border, no modern text

scenes.title: Behold my servant (glory)
scenes.subject_block: a single seamless full-bleed Baroque oil painting with HARD Caravaggio chiaroscuro, heavy impasto and deep dark shadow: a towering overwhelming COLUMN AND BURST OF PURE RADIANT GOLDEN LIGHT pouring down through massive churning storm cloud that fills the whole wide frame, blinding formless glory and god-rays with absolutely NO human figure inside it — NO head, NO face, NO shoulders, NO arms, NO body, NO human shape, NO halo, NO person of any kind, only formless brilliant light; far below on dark ground a small cluster of awe-struck onlookers in plain 1st-century Judean robes gaze up in wonder at the glory above them; overwhelming majesty present but unseen; one continuous horizontal image, no panels, no dividers, no triptych, no frame, no border

scenes.title: The picture breaks (the marred face)
scenes.subject_block: the marred beaten face of the suffering Servant, a man in his early thirties with dark damp hair and a short beard (the same man as the risen Christ, but bruised), his face swollen and bruised, brow furrowed, eyes downcast in deep sorrow, no beauty that we should desire him, heavy shadow with a single cold shaft of light across his wounded face, NO candle, NO elderly man, NO hood

scenes.title: Despised and rejected
scenes.subject_block: a lone suffering figure turned away from by a distant crowd of robed people who look away, isolation, cold grey light

scenes.title: Wounded for our transgressions (the cross beat)
scenes.subject_block: a single seamless full-bleed Baroque oil painting, hard Caravaggio chiaroscuro: a robed crucified Servant on a wooden cross seen close and intimate, his head bowed in sorrow, his nail-pierced hand the clear focal point catching the shaft of light — the hand anatomically correct and clearly painted with five distinct separated fingers and a clearly separate thumb, a single iron nail driven through the open hand with only subtle blood, NOT graphic; dark storm sky behind, reverent, the same dark-haired bearded man as the risen Christ; one continuous image, no frame, no panels, no border, no text
scenes.camera: slow push-in to the pierced hand

scenes.title: The chastisement of our peace
scenes.subject_block: a single seamless full-bleed Baroque oil painting, hard chiaroscuro, on open desolate ground under a heavy sky (NOT a room, NOT an interior): in the foreground center one lone Servant figure crushed low to the earth beneath a massive crushing burden bearing down on his bent back and shoulders, his body buckling under the great dark weight, the single clear focal subject in deep shadow; directly behind and above him several ordinary people stand fully UPRIGHT, unburdened and freed, ALL dressed in plain 1st-century Judean robes, simple tunics and mantles with cloth head-coverings (absolutely NO European peasant clothing, NO white bonnets or caps, NO aprons, NO bodices, NO vests, NO breeches, NO buckled boots), their straight backs and lifted faces bathed in a warm shaft of breaking light, plainly released because HE carries the load that was theirs; stark visual contrast between his bent crushed form in shadow and their upright freed forms in light makes the substitution unmistakable; NO praying hands, NO healing-the-sick scene, NO domestic room; one continuous image, no frame, no panels, no triptych, no border

scenes.title: All we like sheep have gone astray
scenes.subject_block: scattered sheep wandering apart across a darkening hillside, each going its own way, one shepherd-figure bearing them, dusk
scenes.camera: slow pan across the scattered flock

scenes.title: A lamb to the slaughter
scenes.subject_block: a single white lamb led gently on a rope toward an altar, calm and unresisting, soft sorrowful light, sheep before shearers behind

scenes.title: He opened not his mouth
scenes.subject_block: a single seamless full-bleed Baroque oil painting, hard chiaroscuro: the silent Servant standing bound with roped wrists before a group of shadowed accusers in a dim stone hall, dignified and calm, head bowed, making no defence; the accusers are 1st-century Judean temple priests and guards in robes, prayer shawls and cloth head-coverings or turbans (absolutely NO European wide-brim hats, NO 17th-century coats, NO buckled boots), one cold shaft of light on the Servant; one continuous image, no frame, no panels, no border, no text

scenes.title: The nation, the servant Israel
scenes.subject_block: a Baroque oil painting that COMPLETELY FILLS THE ENTIRE FRAME edge to edge, viewed perfectly flat and straight-on as the scene itself (this is NOT a photograph of a canvas, NOT a framed picture hanging on a wall, NO wall, NO visible canvas edges, NO border, NO perspective skew, NO drop shadow): a long column of weary exiled people trudging under a heavy grey sky, hard chiaroscuro, a whole suffering nation on the march, all in plain 1st-century Near-Eastern robes, tunics, mantles and cloth head-coverings, a few leaning on simple wooden walking staffs (NO flag banners, NO standards, NO pennants, NO European peasant clothing, NO caps or bonnets, NO buckled boots), dust and sorrow, a corporate body
scenes.camera: slow lateral track of the column

scenes.title: A rich man's tomb, the sinless one
scenes.subject_block: a Baroque oil painting that fills the entire frame flat and straight-on (NOT a canvas on a wall, NO border, NO frame): the BURIAL of a body at dusk, hard chiaroscuro — mourners in 1st-century robes and head-coverings reverently carrying and lowering a linen-wrapped shrouded body down to a rock-hewn tomb in a stone hillside, lamplight and a fading dusk glow, deep sorrow and stillness, rough-cut stone graves of the rich nearby; the body is still being laid to rest (absolutely NO Christian cross headstones, NO cross markers; do NOT show a large round stone rolled away beside an open dark doorway, NO open or empty glowing tomb mouth, NO resurrection scene)
scenes.camera: slow approach to the tomb mouth

scenes.title: The Ethiopian in his chariot
scenes.subject_block: a single seamless full-bleed Baroque oil painting, hard chiaroscuro: a dark-skinned Ethiopian royal court official, a dignified middle-aged man with a short black beard wearing rich deep-colored robes with gold trim and a wrapped cloth turban, seated reading an open scroll, riding in a simple authentic ancient 1st-century light two-wheeled wooden chariot drawn by two horses on a dry desert road under an open sky, low warm sun, trailing dust (NOT an ornate gilded European carriage, NOT a coach, NOT a covered wagon); one continuous image, no frame, no panels, no border, no text

scenes.title: Philip comes alongside
scenes.subject_block: a single seamless full-bleed Baroque oil painting, hard chiaroscuro: the SAME dark-skinned Ethiopian court official from the reference image, identical robes, gold trim and turban, now seated in his SAME simple 1st-century light two-wheeled wooden chariot with the SAME two horses halted on the dusty desert road; Philip, a 1st-century Judean traveler in a plain robe and sandals, walks up alongside the chariot and the two meet, warm low sun, dust; one continuous image, no frame, no panels, no border, no text

scenes.title: Preached unto him Jesus
scenes.subject_block: a single seamless full-bleed Baroque oil painting, hard Caravaggio chiaroscuro (NOT smooth modern devotional art): Philip standing beside the chariot points the SAME dark-skinned Ethiopian official (identical robes and turban as the reference image) toward a luminous figure of the risen Christ rising in breaking golden light above the open scroll, revelation dawning on the official's awestruck face; one continuous image, no frame, no panels, no border, no text
scenes.camera: slow tilt up into the light

scenes.title: It pleased the LORD (the cross, dark sky)
scenes.subject_block: a single seamless full-bleed Baroque oil painting, hard chiaroscuro: a robed crucified Servant on a cross on a bare hilltop seen wide and small against a vast black thunderous sky, one dramatic shaft of heaven's light breaking through the storm onto him, dark land far below, cosmic scale and dread, reverent and restrained NOT graphic, the gospel pivot, the same dark-haired bearded Christ; one continuous image, no frame, no panels, no border, no text

scenes.title: The chapter turns toward morning
scenes.subject_block: first pale dawn light breaking over a hewn tomb, the heavy stone, dew and quiet, hope at the edge of dark
scenes.camera: slow rise with the dawn light

scenes.title: He shall be satisfied (the risen Servant)
scenes.subject_block: a radiant risen figure standing in golden morning light, alive, scars on his hands, serene and satisfied, glory restored

scenes.title: The arm of the LORD is a person
scenes.subject_block: a mighty outstretched arm of light resolving into the pierced hand and face of Christ, the saving strength of God revealed as a person
scenes.camera: slow reveal from arm to face

scenes.title: It has your name (grace reaches out)
scenes.subject_block: Christ reaching a pierced hand toward the viewer in warm light, one ordinary kneeling figure receiving it, intimate and tender
scenes.camera: slow push-in to the offered hand

scenes.title: His name is Jesus (HERO close)
scenes.subject_block: the risen Christ full of light, face lifted, the marred-now-glorified Servant, the gospel-pivot hero image, luminous gold, reverent close
